title: Fleebog
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Species |image=[[File:Fleebog_trio.jpg]] |name=Fleebog |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Sentient]][[Ewoks (1985) 11|''Ewoks'' (1985) 11]] |class= |subspecies= |races= |height=1 inch |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=[[Color/Legends|Blue]] and red |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor=Yellow |distinctions= *Antennae *Small size *Yellow shell |lifespan= |origin= |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|The Fleebogs! All I know of them is that they dwell in a maze of tunnels just below the forest floor!|translated=1|Chief Chirpa|}} The '''Fleebogs''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of tiny, [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings characterized by [[Color/Legends|blue]] skin, red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and a yellow shell. They inhabited mazes of tunnels; one group of Fleebogs lived below the surface of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]] near the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]] [[City/Legends|settlement]] of [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] in such a habitat. The Endor Fleebog population used domesticated [[Insect/Legends|insects]] as beasts of burden and as lighting for their tunnels. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the Fleebogs of Endor were threatened by the ravenous [[ra-lugg]], an [[Spider/Legends|arachnid]] that had invaded their subterranean territory. To bring their plight to the attention of the Bright Tree Ewoks and thus gain their assistance, the Fleebogs used a type of [[Plant/Legends|plant]] known as a [[damsel flower]] to shrink the Ewoks' [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] down to their size. With the assistance of the princess and the similarly miniaturized Ewoks [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]], the Fleebogs defeated the ra-lugg and learned that even small beings could handle big problems. ==Biology and appearance== {{Quote|It's hard to get anyone to listen to you when you're only an inch high!|translated=1|A Fleebog|}} [[File:Fleebog_shell.jpg|thumb|left|Fleebogs had yellow, spotted shells.]] The Fleebogs were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] whose members were much smaller than most other intelligent beings. They had a roughly [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] posture, although their [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] were short in proportion to their bodies. They had three fingers, one of them an opposable thumb, on each hand, and two pointed toes on each foot. The Fleebogs stood only one inch high, making them shorter than the lower leg of an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]—a species whose members averaged one [[Meter/Legends|meter]] in height''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''—and small enough to fit in an Ewok's palm. Fleebog skin was [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] and [[Color/Legends|blue]], although some specimens had black spots on their arms. The belly and tail were striated and red. The Fleebog back sported a round, yellow shell with black spots. Fleebogs had two thin antennae and two pointed [[Ear/Legends|ears]] on their high-domed heads. Their slanted, pupilless, yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] flanked a wide [[Nose/Legends|nose]]. The broad Fleebog [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] was flexible and expressive, and capable of speaking [[Ewokese/Legends|Ewokese]]. Their minuscule, white [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] were sharp, and two fangs poked out even when the mouth was closed. ==Society and culture== [[File:Fleebog_tunnel.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Fleebogs lived in underground tunnels and used domesticated insects as mounts.]] {{Quote|The ra-lugg! Spring the tunnel defense quickly!|translated=1|A Fleebog|}} Fleebogs lived in subterranean tunnels just below the surface. Their underground passages were large in relation to their diminutive inhabitants; they stood roughly twice the height of the average Fleebog and two or three times the width. These labyrinthine complexes could seem like mazes to outsiders. The Fleebogs on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon]] of [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]] had access to relatively primitive technology. They fashioned doors and gates from sticks tied with [[Rope/Legends|cord]] and used these to block access to tunnels and to create enclosed spaces. They also employed cord to tie up [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. Endor's Fleebogs knew the properties of the [[damsel flower]], a [[Plant/Legends|plant]] whose pollen caused an Ewok to shrink, although the Fleebogs had no idea that the process continued indefinitely until they used that technique on an Ewok firsthand. Fleebogs used domesticated beetle-like [[Insect/Legends|insects]] as [[Unidentified Endor beetle|beasts of burden]] and mounts, and [[fire beetle]]s to illuminate their homes. Endor's Fleebogs spoke Ewokese. The Fleebogs had an inferiority complex due to their minuscule size. They lived in constant fear of larger [[Diet/Legends|predators]]—such as the [[ra-lugg]], an [[Spider/Legends|arachnid]]-like creature with sharp pincers—which led to the development of defensive measures. For example, they used a stick to prop up a platform of twig and string covered in rocks; if the ra-lugg attacked, the twig could be pulled free, strewing about the rocks and blocking off the tunnel. Another tactic was to break through the bottom of a surface puddle to allow the [[Water/Legends|water]] to flood the tunnels below and drown out an intruder. ==History== {{Quote|And Fleebogs, I guess all this shows you that just because you're small doesn't mean you can't handle big problems!»
«Thank you, Princess! And we promise to watch our step from now on!»
«And we'll certainly watch ours!|translated=1|Princess Kneesaa and the Fleebogs, after defeating the ra-lugg|}} [[File:Ewoks_and_Fleebogs_goodbye.png|thumb|left|250px|The Ewoks and Fleebogs established positive relations after defeating the ra-lugg.]] Before the midpoint of [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' places the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated television series in 3.5 ABY but makes no mention of the [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] comic book series. Nevertheless, as the character designs in the comic book are identical to those of the the first season of the ''Ewoks'' television show, the comic book stories must take place around the same time as the animated cartoon. a group of Fleebogs had established a society on the Forest Moon of Endor in the [[Inner Zuma Region]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].{{GamerCite|9|Endor and the Moddell Sector}} At least some of those Fleebogs founded a home in the [[Tree/Legends|forest]] near [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] and made contact with the Ewoks who lived there, although this was brief and unsustained. Nevertheless, the species' numbers never made up more than one percent of the Forest Moon's sentient inhabitants.Although it does not mention the Fleebogs by name, ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' allocates only 1% of Endor's sentient population to species other than Ewoks and Yuzzums. By 3 ABY, Endor's Fleebogs were terrorized by the ra-lugg, a web-laying arachnid predator. Although they had defenses against the creature, the Fleebogs feared themselves inadequate to defeat the beast and thus tried to petition the Bright Tree Ewoks for aid. The Ewoks did not notice the diminutive beings, so their pleas went unanswered. The tiny beings set a trap for [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], the [[Prince/Legends|princess]] of Bright Tree Village, by setting out a damsel flower for her to find. Upon sniffing it, she shrank in size, and a [[Unidentified Fleebog emissary 1|party]] [[Unidentified Fleebog emissary 2|of four]] [[Unidentified Fleebog emissary 3|Fleebog]] [[Unidentified Fleebog emissary 4|emissaries]] kidnapped her. Once the Fleebogs and their captive were underground, the ra-lugg attacked, forcing the Fleebogs to spring their defenses. The event convinced Kintaka of the danger that the small beings faced. An Ewok rescue party—consisting of [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] and [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket W. Warrick]], who had been shrunk down and sent to retrieve the princess—threatened to attack the Fleebogs, but the princess convinced them to aid the small beings instead. However, Kintaka was shrinking at a continuous rate, a consequence that the Fleebogs had not predicted. The Ewoks and Fleebogs thus agreed to retrieve a satchel of [[growing powder]] from the web of the ra-lugg. The princess, now small enough to evade the ra-lugg's notice, retrieved the powder. She scattered some of it on the monster, causing the beast to grow larger and run off in surprise. The Ewoks used the remainder of the substance to return to their normal dimensions, and the Fleebogs learned that size was no impediment to achieving great things. ==Behind the scenes== The Fleebogs were created by writer [[David Manak]] and illustrator [[Warren Kremer]] for the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel]] [[Star Wars: Ewoks (1985)|''Ewoks'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]]. Their only appearance, [[Ewoks (1985) 11|''The Incredible Shrinking Princess'']] in Issue 11, was first published in January [[1987]]. ''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' states that of Endor's many sentient species, only the Ewoks and [[Yuzzum/Legends|Yuzzums]] were native to the Forest Moon.''[[Geonosis and the Outer Rim Worlds]]'' The article "[[Castaways of Endor]]" adds only [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]] to this list and implies that all other sentients on Endor were introduced from other worlds.{{Hyperspace|url=fans/hyperspace/source/feature20081201.html|text=Castaways of Endor|int=Castaways of Endor}} According to the article "[[Endor and the Moddell Sector]]," such introductions were relatively common due to the dual navigational hazards of a massive [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] shadow and a shield of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] debris that crashed countless [[Starship/Legends|starships]] on the moon. ==Appearances== *[[Ewoks (1985) 11|''Ewoks'' (1985) 11]] {{1st}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Fleebogs}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Fleebog }} [[Category:Sentient species of Endor]] {{RelatedCategories |[[Category:Fleebogs]] }}
